subject: CDS UI AAF support
commit_message:
CDS UI AAF support

Add reverse-proxy service to cds-ui deploy
Add forward-proxy service to cds-ui deploy
Add aaf agent as init container for cds-ui
Hardcode the certificate, password for AAF integration
Use sdnc namespace

Issue-ID: CCSDK-706
Change-Id: Ia8fe75b4420375ac6686ab83ef6bb5c6e321f725
Signed-off-by: eeginux<henry.xie@est.tech>
Signed-off-by: gummar <raj.gumma@est.tech>

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Please use automated certificate generation instead of hardcoding the certificate

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> Please use automated certificate generation instead of hardcoding the certificate

The hardcoded certifcates are used by reverse-proxy and forward-proxy, and all the user authentication and authorization are done through AAF. now the CDS-UI is integrated with AAF service for RBAC. 

Automated certifcate generation is done with the init container, you can enable the feature by aaf.enabled flag. The tricky part is to use the auto-generated certifcates/password for each service, revser-proxy, forward-proxy, cds-ui, it involves the certifcate password decryption, obfuscation and configuration. Reverse-proxy and forward-proxy code need some refactoring to support external configuration. cds-ui need to be refactored to support reading certifcate and password as well.

This patch is submitted for the CDS-UI and AAF integation with sidecar implementation. Using the auto-generated certifcate and password would be the next task.

Patch Set 1:

Kyrstof, Using the auto-generated certificate and password would be the next task. Can we proceed with this patch.
